summary: Keeper League has genuine structural and emotional strengths: the five-tamperer architecture is inventive and earns its tonal pivot, the mystery mechanics work without cheating, Mallick's correction-map conceit is the best device in the book, and chapters 16-18 land real emotional weight. The novel's failure profile is predominantly prose-level rather than structural, but the cumulative effect is disqualifying at shortlist level. In ranked order of consequence: (1) Pervasive prose formula recurrence — 'the specific [noun]' appears 30+ times, 'Not X. Y.' negation-correction appears 25+ times, 'the way [pronoun] [verb]' simile appears 75+ times, and em-dash appositives dominate the rhythmic texture of virtually every chapter. Individually these are fine constructions; collectively they produce a mechanical prose signature that undermines the novel's otherwise muscular diction. (2) Comedy register undercommitment — the style bible demands 'crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel' men's dialogue and the manuscript delivers clever, ironic, moderately profane comedy. The gap is significant enough that the novel reads as sanitized against its own stated mode. (3) Late-chapter interiority overstatement — chapters 16-17 especially produce long interior paragraphs that restate insights the scene has already earned, converting dramatic pressure into essayistic analysis. (4) Mechanical motif overuse — loon calls (35+ instances) and refrigerator cycling (40+ instances) function as default scene-punctuation rather than purposeful motif, flattening the environmental texture. (5) Composed dialogue in climactic chapters — several key exchanges in chapters 16-17 produce compressed metaphors and perfectly crafted insights that feel written-to-land rather than spoken under pressure. Fixing these five failure modes would plausibly bring the book to shortlist readiness; the structural architecture, character work, and mystery resolution are strong enough to carry the novel once the prose-level issues are resolved.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4868 — 'Her mother had always said you don't show up to someone's house empty-handed. Even the bad houses. Especially the bad houses.' Compare style_bible.json example_line for detective_mallick: 'I brought a casserole because my mother always said you don't show up empty-handed, even to the worst places. Especially to the worst places.'
findings.problem: Near-verbatim echo of Mallick's NON-COPYABLE calibration example line from the style bible. The sentence preserves the mother's-rule structure, the 'empty-handed' core, and the 'Even/Especially' rhetorical doubling. Constitution rule 2b and the style bible header both mark these lines as non-copyable reference material.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4868. Replace the mother's-rule formulation with a Mallick-specific domestic detail that conveys the same casserole-as-social-protocol idea without echoing the calibration line's structure. The 'Even/Especially' doubling and the 'empty-handed' phrasing must both change.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised line at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4868 conveys Mallick's casserole habit without reproducing the structure, key phrases, or rhetorical pattern of the style bible example line. Fail if the mother's-rule + empty-handed + Even/Especially skeleton remains recognizable.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4636-4668 — Mallick's interior recap reviews the fire document (already shown in chapters 8, 12, and 16), Cliff's phone contents (already shown in chapter 12), the BCA forensic report details (shoes, head wound — already established in chapters 14-15), and the correction map (already shown in chapters 4-5 and 10). Each item is re-presented at full paragraph weight as though newly informative.
findings.problem: Chapter 18 spends 32 lines of dense expository interiority re-teaching the reader the entire evidentiary case — fire document, phone texts, forensic details, correction map — that the reader has already learned across chapters 8, 10, 12, 14-16. While Mallick's competence and complete understanding carry dramatic value, the material is presented as though the reader needs to learn it rather than as confirmation of what the reader already knows. At shortlist level, this recapitulation dilutes the chapter's strongest material: the confession scene and the final morning.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4636-4668. Compress the evidentiary recap to 8-12 lines maximum. Replace full re-teaching with Mallick's evaluative relationship to the evidence — what she concluded from it, what she still doesn't know, what she wants from Tommy — rather than re-presenting the facts themselves. The fire document, phone texts, and forensic report should be referenced by shorthand, not re-narrated. Keep the correction-map detail (lines 4648-4658) tighter but present, since this is the first time the reader sees the full map assembled as a coherent investigative tool.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4636-4668 references the known evidence without re-narrating it at full paragraph weight, and the reader's focus shifts quickly to Mallick's evaluative stance and what she still needs. Fail if the section still re-presents the fire document contents, phone text contents, or forensic details at the same length and informational density as the original.

pattern_findings.global_problem: Three syntactic templates recur so frequently they constitute prose tics rather than style: (a) 'the specific [noun] of/that' appears 30+ times across 15 chapters as a default intensifier; (b) 'Not X. Y.' / 'Not X — Y.' negation-correction appears 25+ times as a rhythmic device for characterizing speech tone or action quality; (c) 'the way [pronoun/noun] [verb]' simile construction appears 75+ times. Constitution rule 9 flags any construction appearing more than four times in the book as a tic. These three templates collectively produce a mechanical prose signature audible across virtually every chapter.
pattern_findings.global_rewrite_principles: Replace 80%+ of 'the specific [noun]' with varied intensifiers, concrete images, or no intensifier at all — the noun is often strong enough alone. Replace 'Not X.' sentence fragments with varied constructions: subordinate clauses, simple declaratives, or merged sentences. Vary 'the way [pronoun] [verb]' similes with direct comparisons, metaphor, or concrete description. Preserve at most 3-4 instances of each template across the entire novel where the construction does its strongest work.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:785 'the specific golden wood of a lake house'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:801 'the specific thing he could not afford'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:841 'the specific helplessness of a man whose job was to control outcomes'
chapter_hits.problem: Three instances of 'the specific [noun]' in a single chapter (180 lines), creating a rhythmic tic in Tommy's interiority. The construction abstracts sensation into a formula rather than rendering it through concrete image.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:785, 801, 841. Keep at most one instance (841 is strongest — the helplessness construction earns the weight). Replace the other two with concrete sensory detail or remove the intensifier. At line 785, 'the specific golden wood' can become simply 'the golden wood' or a more particular image of the pine.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 03 contains at most one 'the specific [noun]' construction and the remaining instances have been replaced with varied phrasing. Fail if two or more instances remain.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:866 'the specific tremor that came from too much caffeine'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:998 'the specific posture of a man who'd just been told something'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1114 'the specific hollow that settled after an interrogation'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1118 omitted long line; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1126 'the specific configuration that twenty-five years of weekends had worn into habit'
chapter_hits.problem: Five instances of 'the specific [noun]' in Joel's chapter — the densest cluster in the manuscript. The repetition flattens Joel's analytical voice into a single syntactic gear.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:866, 998, 1114, 1118, 1126. Keep at most one instance (866 is the strongest — the caffeine tremor is a good physical detail). Replace the others with Joel-specific diction: legal precision, cross-examination rhythms, or concrete observation. At line 1126, 'the specific configuration' can become 'the configuration' or a more particular image of which chairs they chose.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 04 contains at most one 'the specific [noun]' and the revised passages use varied syntactic approaches to intensification. Fail if three or more instances remain.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1753 'the specific way she held her mug with both hands'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1799 omitted long line containing 'the specific'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1897 omitted long line containing 'the specific'
chapter_hits.problem: Three instances of 'the specific' in Doug's chapter, where the construction is particularly damaging because Doug's voice should be the quietest and most observational — mechanical intensifiers undercut his distinctive stillness.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1753, 1799, 1897. Replace all three. Doug's interiority should render sensation through image and omission, not through the narrator's intensifier. At line 1753, 'the specific way she held her mug' can become concrete — show Steph's grip, the steam, the temperature of the memory — without the formula.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 07 contains zero instances of 'the specific [noun]' and Doug's interiority uses concrete image or selective silence instead. Fail if any instances remain.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2464 'the specific'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2518 'the specific shame was'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2496 'Not a conspiracy. A committee of the whole'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2506 'Not a murder. Five tamperings.'
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 10 combines the 'the specific' tic with the 'Not X. Y.' negation-correction pattern. Mallick's analytical voice should produce varied syntactic structures, but the chapter defaults to these two templates for emphasis.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2464, 2518. Replace 'the specific' instances. At lines 2496 and 2506, the 'Not X' constructions work better because they mirror Mallick's case-note style — keep these but ensure the chapter doesn't stack them. Check that no more than two 'Not X' fragments appear within any 50-line span.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if 'the specific' is eliminated from chapter 10 and 'Not X' constructions appear no more than twice within any 50-line window. Fail if the prose-formula density remains unchanged.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4244 'Not a question. Paul said it the way Joel had heard him state final claim numbers'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4292 'Not a flinch, exactly, but Joel saw it register'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4332 'the specific kind of man who would carry another person's worst secret'
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 16 stacks 'Not X' fragments (4244, 4292) and 'the specific' (4332) with 'the way' similes (4244) in a chapter whose dramatic content is strong enough to carry itself without formula. The constructions become visible scaffolding around Joel's deduction.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4244, 4292, 4332. At 4244, let Paul's delivery speak for itself without 'Not a question' — the reader can hear the tone from context. At 4292, replace the 'Not a flinch' fragment with a direct observation. At 4332, drop 'the specific kind of' — 'a man who would carry' is stronger without the formula.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if the three cited constructions are replaced with varied syntax and the chapter's prose no longer stacks these templates within close proximity. Fail if 'Not X' and 'the specific' both still appear.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4395 'Not hostile. Not the coaching volume, not the bluster. Just flat.'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4459 'Not a question. The way he'd write it on a form.'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4495 'the specific kind that comes from holding words back'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4503 'the specific weight of a bond'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4511 'the specific September smell'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4591 'the specific weather that meant September'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4593 'Not a question.'
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 17 is the worst offender in the manuscript — six instances of 'the specific' and three instances of 'Not X' in a single chapter. The density is acute: lines 4491-4511 contain two 'the specific' constructions within 20 lines, and the chapter uses 'Not a question' twice (4459 and 4593). This creates a mechanical prose texture in what should be the novel's most emotionally raw chapter.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4395, 4459, 4495, 4503, 4511, 4591, 4593. Keep at most one 'the specific' (4495 is the strongest — the held-words construction earns it). Eliminate all others. Keep at most one 'Not a question' in the chapter (4459 is more effective because it's Paul's voice). Replace 4593 with a direct observation of Tommy's declarative tone. At 4511, 'the specific September smell' should become concrete — name the decay, the cold, the turned leaves — without the formula.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one 'the specific' and at most one 'Not X' fragment, with all other instances replaced by varied constructions. Fail if two or more of either template remain.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4738 'the specific, particular failure'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4784 'Not the coached stillness'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4800 'Not the staircase'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4856 'Not a word'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4896 'the specific cold of a Minnesota September morning'
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 18 uses 'the specific' twice and 'Not X' three times. The final chapter should land with clarity and earned restraint; formula constructions dilute the closing movement's authority.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4738, 4784, 4800, 4856, 4896. At 4738, 'the specific, particular failure' is doubly modified — cut to concrete image of what Tommy's face showed. At 4896, the novel's final paragraph should not use 'the specific' — render the cold through sensation, not formula. Keep 4800 ('Not the staircase') if it works as Mallick's precise elimination. Replace 4784 and 4856.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 18 contains at most one 'Not X' construction and zero instances of 'the specific,' and the final paragraph renders weather through concrete sensation rather than the formula. Fail if 'the specific' appears in the final paragraph or if 'Not X' appears more than once.

pattern_findings.global_problem: Loon calls (35+ instances) and refrigerator cycling (40+ instances) have crossed from purposeful environmental motif into default scene-punctuation. Many instances function as paragraph-closers or silence-fillers rather than carrying changed meaning or pressure. Constitution rule 9 applies: a descriptive default appearing this frequently is a tic. Some instances earn their presence — Mallick timing the refrigerator, the final loon call closing the novel — but the majority are interchangeable and could be removed without loss.
pattern_findings.global_rewrite_principles: Cut 60-70% of loon-call and refrigerator-cycling instances across the manuscript. Preserve instances where the motif carries changed meaning (Mallick timing it, the loon at novel's end, a character's specific emotional relationship to the sound). Replace removed instances with varied ambient detail — wind, pine, water, or silence itself — or cut the scene-punctuation entirely and let the prose breathe without an environmental coda.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:526 'A loon called from the lake — that sliding, deranged sound'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:574 'a loon called for the second time, that territorial wail'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:610 'The refrigerator cycled. The pines moved'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:630 'as regular as the loons and the refrigerator'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:654 'The refrigerator cycled on'
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 2 uses 3 loon calls and 3 refrigerator references in 220 lines. The first loon call (526) and the observation at 630 earn their place; the rest are mechanical punctuation.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:574, 610, 654. Cut the loon at 574 (the 'second time' meta-commentary makes it self-conscious). At 610, replace refrigerator with a different ambient detail or let the silence stand. At 654, cut or vary — Paul doesn't need a third refrigerator reference.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 02 contains at most one loon call and one refrigerator reference, each carrying distinct emotional or dramatic weight. Fail if three or more combined instances remain.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1562 refrigerator reference; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1596 'A loon called from across the water, the sound arriving across the flat surface'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1690 refrigerator; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1724 'the house was quiet except for the refrigerator'
chapter_hits.problem: Three refrigerator references and one loon call in Nate's chapter. Nate's anxious interiority should be punctuated by his own nervous habits, not by the house's default ambient package.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1562, 1690, 1724. Cut two of the three refrigerator references. Replace with Nate-specific sensory detail — his phone screen, his own breathing, the sounds he's hyper-aware of as a podcaster. Keep 1596 (loon) only if it serves Nate's podcast-brain instinct to process sounds as content.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 06 contains at most one refrigerator reference and uses Nate-specific ambient detail for the remaining scene punctuation. Fail if two or more refrigerator mentions remain.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2446 'the sort of refrigerator that announced itself. Every forty minutes or so — Mallick had timed it'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2488 'The refrigerator cycled on'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2534 'A loon called from somewhere across the water'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2592 'The loons had moved farther across the lake'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2632 'She let the loon answer'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2654 'The refrigerator cycled on inside the kitchen'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2690 'The refrigerator cycled off'

The opening refrigerator-timing passage (2446) is the strongest instance in the book — Mallick's analytical relationship to the appliance is genuine character work. But the subsequent references dilute it into background noise.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2488, 2592, 2632, 2654, 2690. Keep 2446 (Mallick timing it) and one loon (2534, the setup for the interview). Cut or replace the rest. At 2632, 'She let the loon answer' is self-consciously literary — cut entirely.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 10 retains the refrigerator-timing passage at 2446 and at most one loon call, with other instances cut or replaced by varied ambient detail. Fail if three or more combined loon/refrigerator instances remain beyond the kept instances.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3795 'A loon called once from the far shore, low and carrying'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3801 'The old refrigerator clicked on with its forty-minute wheeze'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4013 'The refrigerator clicked off'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4041 'The refrigerator clicked back on. The loon called once more'
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 15 uses loon-refrigerator pairs as default scene-transition markers. Line 4041 pairs both in a single sentence, making the mechanical nature of the device explicit.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3795, 4013, 4041. Keep 3801 (the forty-minute wheeze is Mallick's investigative tool). Cut the loon at 3795 or replace with a different night-dock sensory detail. At 4041, the paired loon-refrigerator sentence should be cut or replaced — the pairing makes the scaffolding visible.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 15 does not pair loon and refrigerator in the same sentence and contains at most one of each as standalone scene detail. Fail if the loon-refrigerator pairing at 4041 remains or if three or more combined instances persist.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4375 'the refrigerator was in the loud phase of its cycle'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4567 'The loons had started again. The tremolo — the alarmed call'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4627 'the loons calling across the water in the dark. The wail this time'
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 17's Paul-Tommy confrontation is the emotional climax of the novel. The loon calls at 4567 and 4627 both describe the call's quality in detail ('the tremolo — the alarmed call' and 'The wail this time — the long, rising one'). This level of loon-call analysis in the novel's most dramatic chapter converts ambient detail into narrated ornament.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4567, 4627. Keep at most one loon reference in the chapter and strip the descriptive elaboration — let the sound be itself, not an annotated field guide entry. At 4567, the parenthetical about what the call means ('sounded like laughing if you didn't know... sounded like warning if you did') is the narrator explaining the motif's symbolism. Cut the explanation. At 4627, 'the wail this time' implies the narrator is categorizing loon calls for the reader. Let the sound arrive without taxonomy.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one loon reference without descriptive elaboration of the call type, and the refrigerator reference at 4375 is either cut or reduced to background. Fail if two or more annotated loon calls remain.

pattern_findings.global_problem: Chapters 16-18 consistently produce long interior paragraphs that restate insights already earned by the scene's concrete action. The pattern: a dramatic beat lands (Paul reads the fire document, Tommy breaks down, Tommy confesses), and then 10-20 lines of narrated interiority translate the beat into thematic analysis, moral inventory, or relational diagnosis. The later pages of each chapter mostly paraphrase what the reader already understood from the action.
pattern_findings.global_rewrite_principles: Cut or compress post-beat interiority by 40-60%. Trust the reader to assemble meaning from scene action, dialogue, and gesture. When a paragraph begins restating what the prior paragraph's action already demonstrated, cut it. When the narrator explains what a character's expression means after showing the expression, cut the explanation.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4264-4266 — Joel's two-paragraph legal analysis of the fire document's evidentiary value. The first paragraph runs to 16+ lines of continuous interior legal reasoning. snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4310 — 'Paul was standing in the hallway of a house...' paragraph restates the chapter's earned insight (Martin's protection system failed) in summary form.
chapter_hits.problem: After Joel and Paul's deduction lands through dialogue (lines 4234-4256), the chapter shifts to Joel's extended legal-essay interiority (4264-4266) that reads as authored analysis rather than close-third thought. At 4310, another long paragraph restates Paul's emotional calculus in summary form after the dialogue has already shown it.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4264-4266. Cut Joel's legal analysis by 50% — keep the core tension (evidence is suggestive but not proof) and cut the second paragraph's restatement of the same point. Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4310. Compress to 3-4 lines or cut — Paul's face and the preceding dialogue have already done this work.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if Joel's legal interiority at 4264-4266 occupies no more than 8 lines and does not restate the same evidentiary assessment twice, and if the Paul paragraph at 4310 either does not exist or adds a genuinely new observation. Fail if the interiority sections still run at their original length with restated analysis.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4377 — 13-line paragraph of Paul standing at the sliding glass door, restating his grief and his ignorance in summary form; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4503-4507 — two paragraphs analyzing the Martin-Tommy bond's nature after Tommy has already said 'He loved you too'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4547 — 6-line paragraph describing Tommy as 'not a loud man or a dangerous man or a coach or a killer or any of the things the surface had said he was,' restating the chapter's central recognition; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4587-4599 — Paul's massive interior paragraph (12+ lines) analyzing Martin's protection system, repeating the word 'management' and restating the chapter's thesis about Paul-vs-Martin approaches to loyalty
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 17 is the novel's emotional climax but spends approximately 40% of its post-confrontation pages restating insights the scene has already earned. The Paul-Tommy dialogue is strong; the narrated interiority following each exchange explains what the dialogue already showed. The chapter produces at least four separate extended restatements of the same core insight: Martin's protection of Tommy was real but unsustainable, and Paul will not repeat it.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4377, 4503-4507, 4547, 4587-4599. At 4377, cut to 5-6 lines — keep the physical detail (Paul at the door, Tommy in the chair) and cut the grief-summary. At 4503-4507, cut the analytical paragraph that follows 'He loved you more' — the sentence stands alone. At 4547, cut entirely — the prior scene has earned this recognition without narrator summary. At 4587-4599, compress by 60% — keep Paul's decision (he will not be Martin) and cut the restatement of why Martin's system failed, which the reader has now heard three times.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if the cited passages are compressed so that no two passages within chapter 17 restate the same thematic insight (Martin's protection was unsustainable / Paul will not repeat it) in substantially similar language, and the chapter's post-confrontation sections total no more than 60% of their original line count. Fail if the chapter still produces three or more separate restatements of the same insight.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4820-4826 — After Tommy is handcuffed, the narration restates what the handcuffs mean ('the wrists of an athlete, of a man who gripped things for a living, who shook hands hard and squeezed shoulders and held tackling dummies and, on a dock three nights ago, held another man's back beneath the surface'). snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4860 — Paragraph-length restatement of what the weekend cost ('They'd come here on a Thursday... they were leaving with one friend dead from a heart that quit, one friend dead in three feet of water, and one friend in the back of a county cruiser'). snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4888-4892 — The draft board meditation restates the novel's central metaphor (the league as a structure that survived everything except what it was actually about).
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 18 is the strongest of the three final chapters but still produces three extended passages that summarize the novel's meaning rather than trusting the scene's images to carry it. The handcuff-wrist passage (4820-4826) and the weekend-cost paragraph (4860) both explain what the reader already understands from the accumulated weight of 17 prior chapters.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4820-4826 — Cut the appositive list after 'wrists' ('of an athlete, of a man who...') and let the handcuffs closing be the image. Revise 4860 — compress the weekend-cost summary to 2-3 lines or cut entirely. Keep 4888-4892 — the draft board meditation is the novel's closing image and earns its weight, but tighten it by cutting the final clause ('except what it was actually about'), which over-explains.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if the handcuff passage at 4820-4826 does not re-catalog Tommy's physical history, the weekend-cost paragraph at 4860 is reduced to at most 3 lines, and the draft board meditation does not state its own thematic conclusion. Fail if all three passages remain at their current length and explicitness.

pattern_findings.global_problem: The style bible's aesthetic risk policy demands 'crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel' men's dialogue when 'there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours.' Tommy's profanity profile is 'very high' with profanity as 'connective tissue, not emphasis.' The manuscript delivers moderately profane, clever, ironic comedy — witty banter about draft strategy, the Viagra subplot played for embarrassment rather than crudeness, and men who talk like sharp literary characters rather than middle-aged men at a lake house after seven beers. The gap between requested register and delivered register is significant enough to constitute comedy sanitization per constitution rules 10 and 53 of the style bible.
pattern_findings.global_rewrite_principles: Roughen dialogue in the comedy chapters to match the style bible's requested register. Add crude sexual humor, casually cruel ribbing, and higher-volume profanity to group scenes. Tommy's dialogue should contain profanity in nearly every sentence during casual group settings. The humor should sometimes be genuinely vulgar — not every joke needs to be clever. Let the men be clumsy, repetitive, and crude in the way that long-established male friendships are crude. The comedy should make the reader laugh and occasionally wince, not admire the wit.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3-447 — The draft scene, which should be the novel's comedic peak, plays as sharp group banter with moderate profanity. The hot tub scene (lines 315-387) is described as happening 'two bodies past the tub's comfortable capacity' but the dialogue during this scene — which the style bible says should be 'crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit' between drunk men in a hot tub — is rendered through narration rather than shown.
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 1 establishes the group dynamic through clever banter rather than the crude, raunchy register the style bible demands. The hot tub scene — the most natural setting for the requested register — is largely summarized. Tommy's dialogue contains some profanity but nowhere near the 'connective tissue' density his profile specifies. The comedy is funny but clean relative to the style bible's ask.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:100-200 (draft banter) and 315-387 (hot tub scene). Increase Tommy's profanity density to match his 'very high' profile — 'fuck' and 'shit' should appear in most of his casual sentences. Add at least two exchanges of genuinely crude humor — sexual jokes, body humor, the casually cruel ribbing that old male friendships produce. Show the hot tub conversation rather than summarizing it. The goal is not vulgarity for its own sake but the specific texture of men who've been drinking together for 25 years.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 01 contains at least three exchanges of crude/raunchy humor that go beyond clever wit, Tommy's dialogue profanity density matches his 'very high' profile, and the hot tub scene includes shown dialogue rather than summarized conversation. Fail if the comedy register remains at its current level of polish and moderate profanity.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:671-861 — Tommy's Viagra chapter. The Viagra subplot has inherent crude-comedy potential that the chapter renders primarily through Tommy's internal embarrassment and physical comedy (the flushing scene). Tommy's dialogue with himself and his thoughts about the Viagra are anxious rather than crude. His style bible says his profanity is 'connective tissue' — here it's occasional emphasis.
chapter_hits.problem: The Viagra chapter plays Tommy's embarrassment straight rather than allowing the crudeness of the situation to surface in his internal monologue and physical reactions. Tommy's thoughts about giving Martin Viagra as a joke should include the crude humor that generated the joke in the first place — the locker-room context, the specific sexual ribbing that would have accompanied the gift. The chapter treats the Viagra as a source of guilt rather than mining the comic-horror of middle-aged male sexuality.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:700-780. Add Tommy's internal crude humor about the Viagra situation — the locker-room jokes that preceded giving it to Martin, the specific sexual context that makes the gift both funny and terrible. Tommy's interiority should include the profane, sexually explicit register of a man who coaches teenage boys and talks about sex the way coaches talk about sex — bluntly, crudely, without nuance. The embarrassment is still the engine; the crude internal monologue is the texture.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if Tommy's Viagra chapter includes crude sexual humor in his internal monologue that reflects the locker-room context of the original joke, and his profanity density matches his style-bible profile. Fail if the chapter still treats the Viagra exclusively as a source of anxious guilt without the crude-comedy dimension.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2099-2443 — The Karen affair reveal. The group's reaction to Joel's revelation that Martin slept with his ex-wife is handled as emotional debate and relational analysis. The style bible says this should be treated partly 'like a fantasy football ethics debate — was it a violation? Is there a statute of limitations?' The chapter delivers this framework but the dialogue stays at the level of considered argument rather than the messy, profane, interruption-heavy register that six drunk men would actually produce when processing this revelation.
chapter_hits.problem: The Karen reveal scene produces dialogue that is too orderly and considered for the subject matter and the characters' emotional states. Real men processing the news that their dead friend slept with another friend's ex-wife would produce overlapping profanity, crude sexual specifics, and defensive humor alongside the genuine emotional content. The chapter gives us the emotional content but sanitizes the delivery.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2150-2400 (the group discussion). Add overlapping dialogue, crude interruptions, and the specific sexual crudeness that men use when discussing infidelity among friends. Tommy should weigh in with coaching-mode moral pronouncements that include profanity. Nate should try to make it funny and fail. The debate should be messier and louder than its current shape. Not every line needs to be crude, but the register should be rougher than considered literary argument.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if the Karen reveal discussion includes at least two genuinely crude/profane exchanges, shows overlapping and interrupting dialogue, and the group's register matches the style bible's description of men who have been drinking and are processing shocking personal information. Fail if the discussion still reads as orderly considered debate.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3139-3449 — The last comedy chapter: the roster debate and final dinner. This chapter is the last opportunity for the full comic register before chapter 14's tonal break. The roster debate should be the novel's most sustained piece of group comedy, with the men's full vocal range on display. The current version is funny but its humor is situational and ironic rather than crude and profane.
chapter_hits.problem: Chapter 13 is well-structured as the last comedy before the tonal break, but the comedy operates at the level of sharp observation and ironic situation rather than the crude, loud, profane register the style bible demands for group scenes with alcohol. The waiver-wire debate should produce the novel's best crude humor — the men using draft metaphors for death, loyalty, and obligation in ways that are both funny and revealing. The current register is too clean for the novel's final comic set piece.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3150-3400 (the roster debate and dinner). Push the comedy toward the crude/raunchy register: more profanity, more sexually inflected draft metaphors, more casually cruel ribbing. The men should be performing their best versions of themselves — loudest, funniest, crudest — because this is the last night and they know it even if they won't say it. The comedy should be funny enough that its absence in chapter 14 registers as genuine loss.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if the roster debate and dinner scene contain sustained crude/profane comedy that matches the style bible's requested register, with Tommy at 'very high' profanity and the group producing at least three exchanges of genuinely raunchy humor. Fail if the comedy remains at its current level of ironic wit without the crude dimension.

pattern_findings.global_problem: In climactic chapters, characters produce compressed metaphors, perfectly crafted insights, and balanced rhetorical constructions that feel written-to-land rather than spoken under pressure. Constitution dialogue rule 8 flags this: 'Characters who routinely produce compressed insights, perfectly timed rhetorical questions, or wry observations that the narrator then admires are performing craft, not speaking. When in doubt between a line that lands cleanly and one that arrives with friction, choose friction.'
pattern_findings.global_rewrite_principles: Roughen polished dialogue by adding friction: false starts, abandoned constructions, imprecise word choices, and lines that arrive clumsily at their meaning. Trust the reader to do the interpretive work rather than having characters produce ready-made formulations. A character's insight should sometimes take two or three attempts to articulate, not land perfectly on the first try.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4238 'The document was the lever... What he actually had was a grenade with the pin out' — Joel produces a perfectly constructed two-part metaphor under emotional pressure; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4290 'Martin's answer to this was to write it down and lock it in a safe and spend twenty-two years making sure nobody opened it. How'd that work out?' — compressed insight with mic-drop rhetorical question; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4296 'We've known the version of him that Martin maintained' — thesis-statement formulation; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4300 'That's not loyalty. That's a load-bearing wall, and it collapsed' — crafted architectural metaphor with perfect timing
chapter_hits.problem: Joel produces four compressed, craft-perfect formulations in a 60-line span. Each one individually is strong; together they reveal a dialogue voice that is composing rather than speaking. A public defender under emotional pressure would reach for these insights less cleanly — the first attempt would miss, the metaphor would be imprecise, the rhetorical question would be genuine rather than mic-drop.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4238, 4290, 4296, 4300. Keep the strongest (4290 — the 'How'd that work out?' lands because it's blunt rather than crafted). Roughen the others: at 4238, let Joel reach for the metaphor and not quite get it — 'He thought he had — I don't know, leverage. What he had was a bomb.' At 4296, make the formulation less thesis-clean — 'We knew what Martin showed us. That's the version.' At 4300, let the metaphor arrive with less architectural precision — 'That's not loyalty, that's a — it's structural, Paul. It was holding everything up and it fell.'
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if Joel's dialogue in the cited span contains no more than one perfectly crafted metaphor, with the remaining lines showing the friction of a man reaching for meaning under pressure rather than producing it cleanly. Fail if three or more polished formulations remain in the 60-line span.

chapter_hits.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4551 'Without that, Deck, I'm just the guy who killed somebody. That's all I am' — Tommy produces a perfectly compressed self-diagnosis that contradicts his style bible's 'short declarative sentences' and his avoidance of abstract language; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4599 'Martin would've given you the rest of your life' — Paul produces a line so compressed and perfectly timed it reads as composed rather than spoken; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4609 'The gift that was also a sentence. The house that was also a monument to a bond that was never friendship, not really' — narrator analysis of the preceding scene that reads as authored thesis rather than close-third Paul
chapter_hits.problem: Tommy's self-diagnosis at 4551 is too articulate for his style bible profile — under extreme stress, his 'sentences collapse into fragments and repetitions,' not into compressed literary self-assessments. Paul's line at 4599 is perfectly timed in a way that real speech under duress is not. The narrator commentary at 4609 admires its own formulation rather than staying in Paul's head.
chapter_hits.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4551 — Let Tommy's self-assessment arrive in his own register: fragmented, repeated, physical. Something like: 'Without Martin, I'm — look, I'm the guy. That's what I am. The guy who — ' and let it trail off. Revise 4599 — Add friction: 'Martin would've — he would've let you live with this forever. He would've just kept — ' let Paul lose the sentence. Revise 4609 — Cut the narrator's 'gift that was also a sentence' formulation. Paul's close-third shouldn't produce this level of literary polishing; stay in sensation and observation.
chapter_hits.acceptance_test: Pass if Tommy's self-diagnosis at 4551 uses fragments and repetition per his style-bible stress profile rather than a compressed literary statement, Paul's line at 4599 arrives with friction rather than perfect compression, and the narrator at 4609 stays in Paul's sensory close-third rather than producing literary thesis. Fail if all three lines remain at their current level of polish.
